1_answer_correctness.justification: Notebook/source: terminal puzzles (sys32 base32 auth, cipher, hash, maze) have deterministic, programmatically-verifiable success states checked by the server's _get_status 'done' flag; success/score is unambiguous and computed in code (tasks.py _find_easiest_unsolved_task).

2_construct_validity.justification: Task page (decorator description) claims 'cross-run learning / progress retention via increasingly complex task versions'; tracing into benchmark.tasks.run_adaptive_learning + infrastructure.run_agent_loop confirms the model is genuinely driven through a real tool-using agent loop (llm.client.chat.completions.create) against escalating generator difficulty, scored by depth solved. Implementation tightly matches the claim; the notebook AttributeError is an environmental version-pin artifact, not penalized.

3_code_quality.justification: Notebook+package: clean modular architecture (separate envs, infrastructure VFS, telemetry, terminal classes with unit/trace tests), uses @kbench.task idiomatically, checkpoint/restore, free-port server, graceful RunResult error handling; well above functional-but-rough.

4_sample_size.justification: Notebook/source: run_adaptive_learning defines 10 generators each yielding multiple seeded terminal tasks across 5 terminal types, far more than 20 effective evaluation items/trials.

5_metric_design.justification: Notebook code computes a single composite progress score (100*generators_passed minus an iterations 'fine'); it is appropriate and somewhat richer than raw accuracy but is one dimension with no second distinct dimension (e.g. calibration/latency) or markdown justification of metric choice.

6_problem_statement.justification: Task-Page prose ONLY: the notebook markdown cells contain only setup instructions ('install libraries', 'add dataset to sys.path', 'run the task'); the only capability statement is the one-line decorator description. No motivation, no research gap, no reader-facing problem statement on the page.

7_technical_documentation.justification: Notebook markdown documents setup steps and the source package has docstrings/inline comments explaining the harness, but the task page lacks dataset-schema, scoring-rule, and metric-interpretation documentation; reproduction requires reading the package code, so coverage is partial.

8_capability_targeting.justification: Task page names a specific capability (cross-run learning/retention) and the notebook escalates difficulty across generators, but there are no explicit control vs. perturbation conditions isolating 'learning' from raw agentic puzzle-solving ability, so the construct is targeted at a domain level without a clean isolating control.

9_item_diversity.justification: Notebook/source: items are explicitly stratified into 10 ordered difficulty generators (T1..T5 terminal types combined in nested/composite specs), enabling a graded performance gradient (which depth a model reaches).

1_answer_correctness.justification: Notebook code: answers are graded by deterministic word-count + regex lexical 'guillotine' (verifiable) PLUS a 4-judge LLM 'senate' at a 75% threshold with named criteria; but the rubric criteria are single yes/no questions per dimension with no anchored score levels, making the judged portion only moderately specified.

2_construct_validity.justification: Task page claims it measures 'AGI metacognition / epistemic exile' (explaining modern concepts within an old epistemic frame); notebook code actually sends a real prompt to the model and judges its response, so it measures a model -- but the heavy format scaffolding (bracket tags, strict 250-400 word window, long forbidden-token lists) means failures are largely driven by instruction-following/format compliance rather than the claimed 'empathy/metacognition', a moderate confound.

3_code_quality.justification: Notebook code is functional with clear sectioning and a real judge/assertion flow, but is repetitive (5 near-duplicate notebooks), uses broad early-returns, no output-parsing fallback beyond a single bracket regex, and hardcodes exact judge model keys that silently no-op if absent.

4_sample_size.justification: Notebook code: each task notebook issues exactly ONE prompt/scenario (one historical figure); even counting all 5 notebooks as one benchmark there are only 5 evaluation items, far below 10.

5_metric_design.justification: Notebook computes two genuinely distinct dimensions -- deterministic structural compliance (word count + lexical bans) and semantic quality via multi-judge senate voting -- combining objective and judged signals; multiple judges mitigate single-judge bias.

6_problem_statement.justification: Task-Page prose (descriptions/docstrings): clearly names the capability ('Epistemic Exile: explain modern concept in period-accurate frame', 'AGI metacognition') with vivid framing, but does not connect to a broader research question or explain what gap it fills vs existing evals.

7_technical_documentation.justification: Both artifacts: docstrings + inline section comments explain the prompt, extraction, and senate logic, but there is no dataset/schema documentation (items are inline prompts), no scoring-edge-case description, and no markdown walkthrough -- partial coverage.

8_capability_targeting.justification: Task page+notebook: targets a named domain (cross-temporal conceptual translation) and even includes English/Spanish/Ge'ez language variants as a quasi-control, but no controlled difficulty gradient or baseline-vs-perturbation design isolates the specific mechanism; performance conflates language ability, format compliance, and conceptual mapping.

9_item_diversity.justification: Notebook/data: the 5 notebooks vary scenario, era, and language (some natural variation across items), but within each there is a single item and there is no labeled difficulty taxonomy or per-condition breakdown reported in code.

1_answer_correctness.justification: Notebook code: every World-Swap problem has a deterministic correct_answer and naive_answer generated programmatically; correctness uses normalized substring matching plus a verbose-correct guard and multi-layer slop detection (keyword/numeric/embedding/token), giving unambiguous verifiable ground truth.

2_construct_validity.justification: Task page claims 'metacognition: inhibitory accuracy + calibrated confidence + selective abstention + tool control'; notebook code operationalizes exactly this -- it sends 600 counterfactual prompts to the model, collects answer+confidence+abstain decisions, and computes meta-d'/d', ACE, selective-prediction and tier-stratified inhibition with Tier-1 scaffold-equalized controls to remove a known confound. Implementation is tightly aligned to the claim.

3_code_quality.justification: Notebook code: clean dataclasses, seeded deterministic generation, protobuf sanitization, _safe_call exception wrapping, fallback code extraction, parallel evaluation with caching, and bootstrap CIs -- robust and modular.

4_sample_size.justification: Notebook code: generate_dataset produces N=600 problems (150/tier x 4 tiers across 4 problem types), well above 20.

5_metric_design.justification: Notebook computes many genuinely distinct dimensions (SDT d'/meta-d', ECE/calibration bins, ACE Brier-with-asymmetric-penalty, selective-prediction EV, tool utility, confidence entropy) with bootstrap CIs; multiple orthogonal dimensions and bias-aware design, justified in markdown headers.

6_problem_statement.justification: Task-Page prose (header markdown): names the capability (metacognition track, the four tasks, primary metric meta-d'/d', failure modes) clearly, but it reads as a spec/summary and does not articulate why this matters or the gap relative to existing metacognition benchmarks.

7_technical_documentation.justification: Both artifacts: notebook has well-labeled section markdown (engine, slop detection, generator, evaluation) and the header summarizes dataset/metrics, but there is no per-column schema or explicit scoring-edge-case documentation beyond code comments -- strong code-level docs, partial methodology docs.

8_capability_targeting.justification: Task page+notebook: targets a specific sub-capability (metacognitive efficiency under prior-override) with an explicit hypothesis, and the notebook uses Tier-1 identity-rule baseline vs Tier-2-4 swapped conditions with scaffold-equalized prompts -- a deliberate control isolating inhibition/metacognition from formatting.

9_item_diversity.justification: Notebook/data: items are explicitly structured into 4 difficulty tiers x 4 problem types, and the code reports tier_accs/tier_confs and inverse-scaling-index per tier -- a labeled taxonomy with per-condition breakdown enabling a gradient.

1_answer_correctness.justification: Notebook code: datasets carry explicit answer fields and some notebooks (task-2-temporal-ambiguity) use a clean 'answer with only the value' + substring check, but the headline temporalbench-eval scores answers against raw internal encodings like 'fast:sts8:d58:v44:8224' that no model can match (0/734), and several notebooks assert only 'predicted is not None' on the authors' own store, so ground-truth verification is fragile/inconsistent.

2_construct_validity.justification: Task page claims it measures LLM temporal reasoning (validity windows vs decay). But the primary/headline evals do NOT measure a model: temporalbenchv4 and temporalbench-v2-pccc accept llm yet never call it -- they build the authors' own TemporalAttentionStore and assert it returns a value, printing 942/942, 734/734, 324/324 by construction. The one notebook that does prompt the model (temporalbench-eval) scores 0/734 because the expected answer is an internal serialized fact string the prompt never asks for. The headline measurement is structurally disconnected from model output.

3_code_quality.justification: Notebook code runs and is moderately organized (dataclasses, store classes, field-fallback parsing), but contains bare except: pass loops, dead commented-out task calls, mock/hardcoded data in the staleness notebook, and self-asserting tasks that take an unused llm parameter -- substantial rough edges.

4_sample_size.justification: Notebook/data: dataset files hold ~2000 questions per version/seed (e.g. v4_seed0: 942 CurrentQuery + 734 OverlapQuery + 324 PastQueryTrap), far above 20 items.

5_metric_design.justification: Notebook code: the model-facing scoring is a single accuracy via brittle substring/token overlap with no justification, and the self-asserting notebooks reduce to a single not-None check; inappropriate metric for the stated task and only one dimension.

6_problem_statement.justification: Task-Page prose: markdown names the capability ('temporal reasoning: validity windows vs decay', task families) and gives some framing/key-findings, but motivation is thin and reads as competition boilerplate without a clear articulated gap; clearer than a one-liner but not compelling/specific.

7_technical_documentation.justification: Both artifacts: notebooks include step markdown and the dataset has a recognizable schema, but there is no documentation of how the internal-encoding answers should be matched, no scoring-edge-case explanation, and the self-asserting design is undocumented as such -- partial and partly misleading.

8_capability_targeting.justification: Task page+notebook: targets a named domain (temporal/point-in-time fact retrieval) with task-family variety (PastQueryTrap, OverlapQuery, DecayTrap), but the items that actually run against a model do not isolate temporal reasoning -- and the headline tasks test the authors' store, so the design does not cleanly attribute model performance to the claimed capability.

9_item_diversity.justification: Notebook/data: items are explicitly organized into multiple task families (AsOfQA, ChangeDetection, CausalQuery, PastQueryTrap, CurrentQuery, OverlapQuery, DecayTrap) across v1-v4 difficulty tiers and per-family loops report counts, providing a labeled taxonomy/gradient structure.

1_answer_correctness.justification: Notebook code: all three sub-benchmarks verify deterministically -- code-golf exec's submissions against hidden asserts, regex-golf uses re.fullmatch against MATCH/DO_NOT_MATCH lists, EGAT computes exact embedding-centroid distances; baselines are pre-validated, giving unambiguous machine-checkable ground truth.

2_construct_validity.justification: Task page claims measuring 'constraint adaptation / syntactic compression / embedding-geometry navigation'; notebook code operationalizes each precisely -- it really prompts the model over multi-trial reprompting loops, executes its outputs, and scores fractional character reduction or CoM convergence. Failures reflect the targeted skill (compression / geometric reasoning under feedback), tightly aligned with the claim.

3_code_quality.justification: Notebook code: clean, well-commented, robust -- option-block regex extraction with fallbacks, fresh-namespace exec with traceback limiting, system-role-rejection detection and baked-prompt restart, consecutive-API-error backoff, whitespace-token normalization for tokenizer fairness, and JSON trace dumps.

4_sample_size.justification: Notebook code: code-golf has 5 tasks, regex-golf 5 sessions, EGAT 5 prompts (15 distinct items total across the three notebooks, each x10 reprompt trials). Counting evaluation items this sits in the 10-20 band, adequate for large differences but limited granularity.

5_metric_design.justification: Notebook code: uses a well-justified continuous fractional-improvement metric (reduction vs baseline / CoM distance reduction) rather than bare accuracy, with explicit penalty (-1.0) handling, max-over-trials tracking, and per-task aggregation documented in markdown; distinct task-appropriate dimensions across the three sub-benchmarks.

6_problem_statement.justification: Task-Page prose (markdown headers): each notebook clearly states the capability, scoring formula, and a task/session overview table; well written, but motivation for why these skills matter / the gap vs existing evals is not articulated, keeping it at level 1.

7_technical_documentation.justification: Both artifacts: task-page markdown documents the dataset/task table, scoring rule, and config flags, and the notebook has thorough section-by-section comments (dataset, evaluator helpers, task loop, run) explaining design choices -- comprehensive across both artifacts.

8_capability_targeting.justification: Task page+notebook: probes specific well-defined sub-capabilities (character-level code/regex compression; geometric token routing in a third-party embedding space) with a clear hypothesis, multi-trial feedback to expose adaptation, and graded item difficulty (baseline char counts; centroid-distance prompts) acting as a difficulty gradient.

9_item_diversity.justification: Notebook/data: items are explicitly organized with labeled difficulty/structure -- code-golf tasks span increasing baseline-char complexity, regex sessions are titled by constraint type, EGAT prompts escalate token-set complexity; per-item scores are reported separately, enabling a performance gradient.
